element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Each class has distinct weapons, speed, durability characteristics affecting entire playthrough
Non-linear navigation between central hub and side levels; puzzle solving requires revisiting areas with collected items/switches
Themed combat environments with escalating challenges
Open navigation contrasting with dungeon confinement
Complex multi-room structure with large-scale spatial puzzles

element_introduction_order.element: Inventory system items (Disc of Repulsion, Icon of the Defender)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Class-specific item behavior; defensive and utility applications
Fighter: close-quarters physical; Mage: long-range spells; Cleric: hybrid melee/ranged
Requires backtracking and inventory management across connected levels

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching hub safe zones between side levels
Transitioning to wilderness regions from dungeons
typical_tension_triggers: Narrow seminary corridors with elevation changes
Monster spawning events triggered by action scripts
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive escalation with hub-based breathing room; non-linear exploration allows player-controlled tension management through level selection

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Side-level connections from hub allow optional exploration; non-linear design encourages revisiting areas after acquiring new items/abilities

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to chosen character class mechanics and basic hub navigation; elemental dungeons begin escalation
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Wilderness and seminary regions expand spatial complexity; puzzle sequences require multi-step item collection and hub traversal
difficulty_curve.late_game: Castle and necropolis present large-scale spatial challenges and intensive combat encounters leading to Korax confrontation
notable_spikes: Transition from introductory hub areas to first elemental dungeon
Necropolis final approach sequence before Serpent Rider Korax battle

notable_level_patterns.description: Central hub level connects to multiple side levels; progression requires solving puzzles across interconnected spaces, collecting items/activating switches, then returning to hub to advance
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Generic hub progression (non-specific in source material)

notable_level_patterns.description: Elemental dungeons → wilderness → seminary → castle → necropolis creates narrative and mechanical escalation across distinct spatial archetypes
Mountainside seminary introduces elevation-based navigation and combat distinct from flat dungeon layouts
Castle and necropolis feature expansive interconnected spaces requiring navigation memory and tactical planning

sources_used: Wikipedia - Hexen: Beyond Heretic (Plot, Gameplay, Development sections)
